summary:
The formation of dental biofilm begins when proteins found within saliva adhere onto tooth surfaces creating an environment conducive both protective minerals deposition yet potentially enabling microbial attachment leading up full maturation stages involving other factors like calcification not addressed here initially during initiation phase focusing solely upon protein layer establishment via saliva components primarily comprising acidic glycopeptides facilitating subsequent steps including potential pathogenic consequences such increased risk caries development among others depending further environmental conditions present thereafter influencing overall structure stability longevity etc...
